observed_gaps.description: define.md/architecture.md/uxflow.md never specified an output-file location convention, unlike discovery.md's explicit docs/wingman/discovery/<slug>.md path — had to improvise where to write requirements/architecture/UX-flow output.
observed_gaps.evidence: plugins/wingman/commands/define.md had no path instruction anywhere in its body prior to this fix; same for architecture.md and uxflow.md.

observed_gaps.description: management-board-activation's per-stage 'Relevant to' gating on manager creation can permanently starve mgr-product/mgr-research, whose only relevant stage (discovery) typically runs once, early, before the 3+ conditional-department threshold is usually crossed.
observed_gaps.evidence: Threshold crossed at Build in this run; discovery never ran again; mgr-product and mgr-research stayed uncreated for the rest of the project's life under the old creation-gating logic.

observed_gaps.description: This sandbox's Agent tool cannot dispatch named dept-*/mgr-*/boardroom-* subagent types at all (a known, previously-documented sandbox limitation, not a new plugin gap) — every dispatch fell back to a general-purpose agent loaded with the real persona file content.
observed_gaps.evidence: Agent({subagent_type: 'dept-product'}) and similar calls errored with 'Agent type not found. Available agents: claude, claude-code-guide, Explore, general-purpose, Plan, statusline-setup'.

shortcuts_disclosed: AskUserQuestion tool unavailable in this sandbox — founder decisions made as explicit stood-in reasoning.
git push skipped — fixture has no configured remote.

hooks/hooks.json never loaded in this execution context, so no hook fired on any Write/Edit/Bash call — dod-structural-gate.mjs and boardroom-checkpoint.mjs could not be empirically exercised in this run.
No live Prisma datasource in the fixture — the POST handler's DB wiring is an explicit disclosed OPEN item in the threat register, not silently treated as done.

Build-stage and Ship-stage Boardroom passes were not both full synchronous 8-seat re-dispatches — Ship reused Build's evidence rather than a fresh pass, a procedural shortcoming of this run's execution, not the plugin's instructions.
real_findings: Planning Milestone Boardroom dispatch returned a real DO NOT SHIP (CTO+CISO both NO_GO: no charge ID to refund against, no idempotency protection) — fixed and re-reviewed to GO_WITH_CONCERNS.

Build-diff Boardroom dispatch caught a real, independent bug: the schema migration would have backfilled every existing customer's userId to '', permanently locking them out of their own cancel button — fixed live, re-tested, 14/14 passing.
classification_outcome: Contributed 2 of the 5 total gaps classified via dogfood-gap-classification this run (output-file convention, mgr starvation) plus 1 already-known sandbox limitation logged as out-of-scope. See the simple-path run record for the other 2 and the full classification outcome.
